name: WalletConnect
what_it_does: Provides an open protocol and cloud relay infrastructure for connecting decentralized applications to 300+ crypto wallets via QR codes, deep links, and the Web3Modal UI component.
use_cases: Add 'Connect Wallet' functionality to a dApp so users can authenticate with MetaMask, Rainbow, Trust Wallet, and 300+ others
Trigger on-chain transactions and message signing from a dApp with user approval through their mobile wallet
Build multi-chain dApps that support Ethereum, Polygon, Solana, and other EVM and non-EVM chains through a unified connection layer
Embed Web3Modal — the pre-built connect wallet UI — to skip custom wallet picker development
Relay wallet session state between desktop dApps and mobile wallets for seamless cross-device UX
not_for: Backend-only agents that need to execute transactions autonomously without user wallet approval — WalletConnect requires human wallet interaction
Projects needing custodial wallet management or programmatic key control without user involvement
best_when: Best when building user-facing dApps that need broad wallet compatibility and a polished connect-wallet UX without implementing per-wallet integrations manually.
avoid_when: Avoid when your agent or automation needs to sign transactions programmatically without user interaction, as WalletConnect is designed around interactive wallet sessions.

auth.notes: Project ID obtained from WalletConnect Cloud dashboard. Used to identify your app against the relay infrastructure and enforce per-project rate limits.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 500,000 monthly active wallets on free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing available for higher MAW volumes and SLA guarantees; contact sales
pricing.notes: Open-source protocol with cloud relay service. Self-hosting the relay is technically possible but not officially supported for production.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits enforced per Project ID; specific thresholds documented in WalletConnect Cloud dashboard

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Connection errors surface as event-driven callbacks rather than synchronous exceptions, requiring event listener patterns that can be tricky to orchestrate in agent workflows.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Session establishment is stateful and not idempotent; existing sessions can be restored but new pairing URIs must be regenerated on reconnect.
known_agent_gotchas: Session URIs (pairing codes) expire after a short window — agents presenting QR codes must handle expiry and regeneration gracefully
WalletConnect v1 is deprecated; projects still on v1 will encounter connection failures as wallet support is dropped
Event-based architecture means agents must manage async session lifecycle (connected, disconnected, request approved/rejected) rather than a simple request-response pattern

security_score_components.security_notes: End-to-end encrypted sessions using symmetric keys derived per session. Project ID is low-sensitivity; real security is in wallet-side signing. Active open-source audit history.
